𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty-Four — Part Four

Divrei HaYamim Bet

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET

Covenant History — The Later Kings of Yehudah and the Exile

Chapters 19 through 36

We Do Not Know What to Do But Our Eyes Are on You — If My People Humble Themselves and Pray — Because Your Heart Was Tender — Let Him Go Up

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Orit Ge'ez — Ethiopian Complete Canon

Masoretic Text

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTERS 19 THROUGH 28 — THE COVENANT KINGS

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 19 Yehoshafat Returned Safely — Should You Help the Wicked — Yehoshafat Went Out Among the People — He Appointed Judges — Consider What You Do — There Is No Injustice With YHWH

1 Yehoshafat the king of Yehudah returned in safety to his house in Yerushalayim.

2 But Yehu the son of Khanani the seer went out to meet him and said to King Yehoshafat, Should you help the wicked and love those who hate YHWH? Because of this, wrath has gone out against you from YHWH.

3 Nevertheless, some good is found in you, for you destroyed the Asheroth out of the land, and have set your heart to seek Creator.

4 Yehoshafat lived at Yerushalayim. And he went out again among the people, from Be'er-Sheva to the hill country of Efrayim, and brought them back to YHWH, the Creator of their fathers.

5 He appointed judges in the land in all the fortified cities of Yehudah, city by city,

6 And said to the judges, Consider what you do, for you judge not for man but for YHWH. He is with you in giving judgment.

7 Now then, let the fear of YHWH be upon you. Be careful what you do, for there is no injustice with YHWH our Creator, or partiality or taking bribes.

8 Moreover, in Yerushalayim Yehoshafat appointed certain Levites and priests and heads of fathers' houses of Yisra'EL, to give judgment for YHWH and to decide disputed cases. They had their seat at Yerushalayim.

9 And he charged them, Thus you shall do in the fear of YHWH, in faithfulness, and with your whole heart.

10 Whenever a case comes to you from your brothers who live in their cities, concerning bloodshed, law or commandment, statutes or rules, you shall warn them, that they may not incur guilt before YHWH and wrath may not come upon you and your brothers. Thus you shall do, and you will not incur guilt.

11 And behold, Amaryahu the chief priest is over you in all matters of YHWH, and Zevadyahu the son of Yishma'el, the governor of the house of Yehudah, in all the king's matters, and the Levites will serve you as officers. Deal courageously, and may YHWH be with the upright.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 19 documents Yehoshafat's covenant judicial reform — the establishment of a national court system in every city of Yehudah with a supreme court in Yerushalayim. The theological foundation of the judicial system is the covenant judge's accountability to YHWH: you judge not for man but for YHWH. Covenant justice is divine administration through human agents.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full. Verse 6-7 — consider what you do, for you judge not for man but for YHWH. He is with you in giving judgment. Now then, let the fear of YHWH be upon you. Be careful what you do, for there is no injustice with YHWH our Creator, or partiality or taking bribes. The judge who understands that he operates under YHWH's direct observation cannot accept bribes or show partiality without direct offense against the Creator.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 19 in full. Verse 2-3 — should you help the wicked and love those who hate YHWH? Nevertheless, some good is found in you. The prophetic rebuke and acknowledgment of genuine covenant good: the prophet does not give Yehoshafat only condemnation. The balanced prophetic assessment — rebuke for failure, acknowledgment of genuine good — is the model of honest pastoral engagement.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10 — you shall warn them, that they may not incur guilt before YHWH and wrath may not come upon you and your brothers. The judicial warning requirement: the judges are not merely to decide cases but to warn the community when their practices will bring guilt before YHWH. The judicial function is simultaneously legal and pastoral.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 20 A Great Multitude Is Coming — We Do Not Know What to Do But Our Eyes Are on You — The Spirit of YHWH Came Upon Yakhazi'el — Stand Still and See the Salvation of YHWH — Yehoshafat Appointed Singers — YHWH Set an Ambush — The Valley of Berekhah

1 After this the Moabites and Ammonites, and with them some of the Me'unites, came against Yehoshafat for battle.

2 Some men came and told Yehoshafat, A great multitude is coming against you from Edom, from beyond the sea, and, behold, they are in Khatzetzon-Tamar, that is, En-Gedi.

3 Then Yehoshafat was afraid and set his face to seek YHWH, and proclaimed a fast throughout all Yehudah.

4 And Yehudah assembled to seek help from YHWH. From all the cities of Yehudah they came to seek YHWH.

5 And Yehoshafat stood in the assembly of Yehudah and Yerushalayim, in the house of YHWH, before the new court,

6 And said, O YHWH, Creator of our fathers, are you not Creator in heaven? You rule over all the kingdoms of the nations. In your hand are power and might, so that none is able to withstand you.

7 Did you not, our Creator, drive out the inhabitants of this land before your people Yisra'EL, and give it forever to the descendants of Avraham your friend?

8 And they have lived in it and have built for you in it a sanctuary for your name, saying,

9 If disaster comes upon us, the sword, judgment, or pestilence, or famine, we will stand before this house and before you — for your name is in this house — and cry out to you in our affliction, and you will hear and save.

10 And now behold, the men of Ammon and Moav and Mount Se'ir, whom you would not let Yisra'EL invade when they came from the land of Egypt, and whom they avoided and did not destroy —

11 Behold, they reward us by coming to drive us out of your possession, which you have given us to inherit.

12 O our Creator, will you not execute judgment on them? For we are powerless against this great horde that is coming against us. We do not know what to do, but our eyes are on you.

13 Meanwhile all Yehudah stood before YHWH, with their little ones, their wives, and their children.

14 And the Spirit of YHWH came upon Yakhazi'el the son of Zekhariyahu, son of Benayahu, son of Ye'iel, son of Mattanyahu, a Levite of the sons of Asaf, in the midst of the assembly.

15 And he said, Listen, all Yehudah and inhabitants of Yerushalayim and King Yehoshafat. Thus says YHWH to you, Do not be afraid and do not be dismayed at this great horde, for the battle is not yours but Creator's.

16 Tomorrow go down against them. Behold, they will come up by the ascent of Tzitz. You will find them at the end of the valley, east of the wilderness of Yeru'el.

17 You will not need to fight in this battle. Stand firm, hold your position, and see the salvation of YHWH on your behalf, O Yehudah and Yerushalayim. Do not be afraid and do not be dismayed. Tomorrow go out against them, and YHWH will be with you.

18 Then Yehoshafat bowed his head with his face to the ground, and all Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim fell down before YHWH, worshiping YHWH.

19 And the Levites, of the Kohathites and the Korahites, stood up to praise YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, with a very loud voice.

20 And they rose early in the morning and went out into the wilderness of Tekoa. And as they went out, Yehoshafat stood and said, Hear me, Yehudah and inhabitants of Yerushalayim. Believe in YHWH your Creator, and you will be established. Believe his prophets, and you will succeed.

21 And when he had taken counsel with the people, he appointed those who were to sing to YHWH and praise him in holy attire, as they went before the army, and say, Give thanks to YHWH, for his steadfast love endures forever.

22 And when they began to sing and praise, YHWH set an ambush against the men of Ammon, Moav, and Mount Se'ir, who had come against Yehudah, so that they were routed.

23 For the men of Ammon and Moav rose against the inhabitants of Mount Se'ir, devoting them to destruction, and when they had made an end of the inhabitants of Se'ir, they all helped to destroy one another.

24 When Yehudah came to the watchtower of the wilderness, they looked toward the horde, and behold, there were dead bodies lying on the ground. None had escaped.

25 When Yehoshafat and his people came to take their spoil, they found among them, in great numbers, goods, clothing, and precious things, which they took for themselves until they could carry no more. They were three days in taking the spoil, it was so much.

26 On the fourth day they assembled in the Valley of Berekhah, for there they blessed YHWH. Therefore the name of that place has been called the Valley of Berekhah to this day.

27 Then all the men of Yehudah and Yerushalayim, with Yehoshafat at their head, returned to Yerushalayim with joy, for YHWH had made them rejoice over their enemies.

28 They came to Yerushalayim, to the house of YHWH, with harps and lyres and trumpets.

29 And the fear of Creator came on all the kingdoms of the countries when they heard that YHWH had fought against the enemies of Yisra'EL.

30 So the realm of Yehoshafat was quiet, for his Creator gave him rest on every side.

31 Thus Yehoshafat reigned over Yehudah. He was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-five years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Azuvah the daughter of Shilkhi.

32 He walked in the way of his father Asa and did not turn aside from it, doing what was right in the sight of YHWH.

33 The high places, however, were not taken away. The people had not yet set their hearts upon the Creator of their fathers.

34 Now the rest of the acts of Yehoshafat, from first to last, are written in the chronicles of Yehu the son of Khanani, which are recorded in the Book of the Kings of Yisra'EL.

35 After this Yehoshafat king of Yehudah joined with Akhazyahu king of Yisra'EL, who acted wickedly.

36 He joined him in building ships to go to Tarshish, and they built the ships in Etzyon-Gever.

37 Then Eli'ezer the son of Dodavahu of Mareshah prophesied against Yehoshafat, saying, Because you have joined with Akhazyahu, YHWH will destroy what you have made. And the ships were wrecked and were not able to go to Tarshish.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 20 is the theological summit of the Yehoshafat narrative. Verse 12 — we do not know what to do, but our eyes are on you — is the most transparent covenant prayer of the kings of Yehudah: honest helplessness combined with total orientation toward YHWH. The battle won by singing is the most striking military narrative in Divrei HaYamim.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full. Verse 21 — he appointed those who were to sing to YHWH and praise him in holy attire, as they went before the army, and say, Give thanks to YHWH, for his steadfast love endures forever. The military formation with singers at the front: the community goes into battle led by praise. The defeat of the enemy begins with the community lifting the covenant refrain of steadfast love.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 20 in full. Verse 20 — believe in YHWH your Creator, and you will be established. Believe his prophets, and you will succeed. Yehoshafat's speech to the army is the most compact theology of faith and prophetic authority in the historical narrative: believing YHWH produces stability; believing the prophets produces success.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22 — when they began to sing and praise, YHWH set an ambush. The precise timing: the military victory begins at the moment the singing begins. The covenant between praise and divine action is the most direct illustration of the principle established in Chapter 5 — YHWH fills the temple when the community sings for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 21 Yehoram Became King — He Killed His Brothers — He Did Evil — Eliyahu Sent Him a Letter — YHWH Struck Him With an Incurable Disease — He Died With No One's Regret

1 Yehoshafat slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of Dawid, and Yehoram his son reigned in his place.

2 He had brothers, the sons of Yehoshafat: Azaryahu, Yekhiel, Zekhariyahu, Azaryahu, Mikha'el, and Shefatyahu. All these were the sons of Yehoshafat king of Yisra'EL.

3 Their father gave them great gifts of silver, gold, and valuable possessions, together with fortified cities in Yehudah, but he gave the kingdom to Yehoram, because he was the firstborn.

4 When Yehoram had ascended the throne of his father and was established, he killed all his brothers with the sword, and also some of the princes of Yisra'EL.

5 Yehoram was thirty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned eight years in Yerushalayim.

6 And he walked in the way of the kings of Yisra'EL, as the house of Akhav had done, for the daughter of Akhav was his wife. And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH.

7 Yet YHWH was not willing to destroy the house of Dawid, because of the covenant that he had made with Dawid, and since he had promised to give a lamp to him and to his sons forever.

8 In his days Edom revolted from the rule of Yehudah and set up a king of their own.

9 Then Yehoram crossed over with his commanders and all his chariots, and he rose by night and struck the Edomites who had surrounded him and his chariot commanders.

10 So Edom revolted from the rule of Yehudah to this day. At that time Livnah also revolted from his rule, because he had forsaken YHWH, the Creator of his fathers.

11 Moreover, he made high places in the hill country of Yehudah and led the inhabitants of Yerushalayim into whoredom and made Yehudah go astray.

12 And a letter came to him from Eliyahu the prophet, saying, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Dawid your father, Because you have not walked in the ways of your father Yehoshafat or in the ways of Asa king of Yehudah,

13 But have walked in the way of the kings of Yisra'EL and have enticed Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim into whoredom, as the house of Akhav led Yisra'EL into whoredom, and also you have killed your brothers, of your father's house, who were better than you,

14 Behold, YHWH will bring a great plague on your people, your children, your wives, and all your possessions,

15 And you yourself will have a severe sickness with a disease of your bowels, until your bowels come out because of the disease, day by day.

16 And YHWH stirred up against Yehoram the spirit of the Pelishtim and of the Arabians who are near the Kushites.

17 And they came up against Yehudah and invaded it and carried away all the possessions they found that belonged to the king's house, and also his sons and his wives, so that no son was left to him except Yeho'ahaz, his youngest son.

18 And after all this YHWH struck him in his bowels with an incurable disease.

19 In the course of time, at the end of two years, his bowels came out because of the disease, and he died in great agony. His people made no fire in his honor, like the fires made for his fathers.

20 He was thirty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned eight years in Yerushalayim. And he departed with no one's regret. They buried him in the city of Dawid, but not in the tombs of the kings.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 21 is the nadir of the Yehoshafat legacy: his son Yehoram murders his brothers, follows Akhav's path, and receives the judgment of Eliyahu's letter — the only written prophetic communication in Divrei HaYamim. The dynasty is preserved only by the unconditional element of the Davidic covenant — verse 7.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full. Verse 7 — YHWH was not willing to destroy the house of Dawid, because of the covenant that he had made with Dawid, and since he had promised to give a lamp to him and to his sons forever. The Davidic covenant functions as the life-preserving element even when the king is apostate: the dynasty survives Yehoram's wickedness because of the promise to Dawid, not because of Yehoram's character.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 21 in full. Verse 20 — he departed with no one's regret. The epitaph of the worst king of Yehudah to this point in the narrative: the community's grief at the king's death is the measure of the king's service to the community. Yehoram receives no mourning because he gave the community no reason to mourn him.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12 — a letter came to him from Eliyahu the prophet. The appearance of Eliyahu in the Yehudah narrative underscores the gravity of Yehoram's alignment with the Akhavide pattern. The prophet whose ministry defined the battle against Akhav's apostasy in the north sends a written oracle to the Akhav's son-in-law in the south.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 22 Akhazyahu Became King — He Walked in the Ways of the House of Akhav — He Was Destroyed by Creator — Atalyah Saw That Her Son Was Dead — She Destroyed All the Royal Family — Yehosheva Hid Yoash

1 And the inhabitants of Yerushalayim made Akhazyahu his youngest son king in his place, for the band of men that came with the Arabians to the camp had killed all the older sons. So Akhazyahu the son of Yehoram king of Yehudah reigned.

2 Akhazyahu was forty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned one year in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Atalyahu the granddaughter of Omri.

3 He also walked in the ways of the house of Akhav, for his mother was his counselor in doing wickedly.

4 He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, as the house of Akhav had done. For after the death of his father they were his counselors, to his undoing.

5 He even followed their counsel and went with Yehoram the son of Akhav king of Yisra'EL to make war against Khaza'el king of Syria at Ramot-Gil'ad. And the Syrians wounded Yoram.

6 And he returned to be healed in Yizre'el of the wounds that he had received at Ramah, when he fought against Khaza'el king of Syria. And Azaryahu the son of Yehoram king of Yehudah went down to see Yehoram the son of Akhav in Yizre'el, because he was wounded.

7 But it was ordained by Creator that the downfall of Akhazyahu should come about through his going to visit Yoram. For when he came there, he went out with Yehoram to meet Yehu the son of Nimshi, whom YHWH had anointed to destroy the house of Akhav.

8 And when Yehu was executing judgment on the house of Akhav, he met the princes of Yehudah and the sons of Akhazyahu's brothers, who attended Akhazyahu, and he killed them.

9 He searched for Akhazyahu, and he was captured while hiding in Samaria, and he was brought to Yehu and put to death. They buried him, for they said, He is the grandson of Yehoshafat, who sought YHWH with all his heart. And the house of Akhazyahu had no one able to rule the kingdom.

10 Now when Atalyahu the mother of Akhazyahu saw that her son was dead, she arose and destroyed all the royal family of the house of Yehudah.

11 But Yehosheva, the daughter of the king, took Yoash the son of Akhazyahu and stole him away from among the king's sons who were about to be put to death, and she put him and his nurse in a bedroom. Thus Yehosheva, the daughter of King Yehoram and wife of Yehoyada the priest, hid him from Atalyahu, so that she did not put him to death.

12 And he remained with them six years, hidden in the house of Creator, while Atalyahu reigned over the land.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 22 brings the Akhavide contamination of the Davidic line to its crisis point: Atalyah seizes the throne and attempts to exterminate the entire Davidic line. The survival of Yoash — hidden in the temple for six years — is the survival of the Davidic covenant promise at its most vulnerable moment.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full. Verse 11 — Yehosheva stole him away from among the king's sons who were about to be put to death, and she put him and his nurse in a bedroom. The courage of the woman who preserves the Davidic line: Yehosheva's act of concealment saves the community's future. The preservation of the covenant depends on this single woman's courage.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 22 in full. Verse 9 — they buried him, for they said, He is the grandson of Yehoshafat, who sought YHWH with all his heart. The posthumous honor given to Akhazyahu because of his grandfather: even the enemy Yehu honors the connection to Yehoshafat's reputation. The legacy of a faithful ancestor provides benefit even to unfaithful descendants.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — it was ordained by Creator that the downfall of Akhazyahu should come about through his going to visit Yoram. The theological interpretation: what appears to be a coincidental visit is divinely ordained as the mechanism of Akhazyahu's judgment. The compiler reads providence behind every historical encounter.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 23 Yehoyada Made a Covenant With the Commanders — Let No One Enter the House of YHWH Except the Priests — They Crowned Yoash — Long Live the King — Atalyah Was Put to Death — Yehoyada Made a Covenant Between YHWH and the King

1 But in the seventh year Yehoyada took courage and entered into a covenant with the commanders of hundreds, Azaryahu the son of Yerokham, Yishma'el the son of Yehohanan, Azaryahu the son of Oved, Ma'aseiyahu the son of Adayah, and Elishafat the son of Zikhri.

2 And they went about through Yehudah and gathered the Levites from all the cities of Yehudah, and the heads of fathers' houses of Yisra'EL, and they came to Yerushalayim.

3 And all the assembly made a covenant with the king in the house of Creator. And Yehoyada said to them, Behold, the king's son. Let him reign, as YHWH spoke concerning the sons of Dawid.

4 This is the thing that you shall do: one third of you, those who come off duty on the Shabbat, of the priests and Levites, shall be gatekeepers of the thresholds.

5 And one third shall be at the king's house, and one third at the Gate of the Foundation. And all the people shall be in the courts of the house of YHWH.

6 Let no one enter the house of YHWH except the priests and ministering Levites. They may enter, for they are holy, but all the people shall keep the charge of YHWH.

7 The Levites shall surround the king, each with his weapons in his hand. And whoever enters the house shall be put to death. Be with the king when he comes in and when he goes out.

8 The Levites and all Yehudah did according to all that Yehoyada the priest commanded. They each brought his men, who were to come off duty on the Shabbat, with those who were to come on duty on the Shabbat, for Yehoyada the priest did not dismiss the divisions.

9 And Yehoyada the priest gave to the commanders the spears and the large and small shields that had been King Dawid's, which were in the house of Creator.

10 And he set all the people as a guard for the king, every man with his weapon in his hand, from the south side of the house to the north side of the house, around the altar and the house.

11 Then they brought out the king's son and put the crown on him and gave him the testimony and made him king. And Yehoyada and his sons anointed him and said, Long live the king.

12 When Atalyahu heard the noise of the people running and praising the king, she went into the house of YHWH to the people.

13 And when she looked, there was the king standing by his pillar at the entrance, and the commanders and the trumpeters beside the king, and all the people of the land rejoicing and blowing trumpets, and the singers with their musical instruments leading in the celebration. And Atalyahu tore her clothes and cried, Treason, treason.

14 Then Yehoyada the priest brought out the commanders who were set over the army, saying to them, Bring her out between the ranks, and anyone who follows her is to be put to death with the sword. For the priest said, Do not put her to death in the house of YHWH.

15 And they seized her, and she went into the entrance of the Horse Gate of the king's house, and they put her to death there.

16 And Yehoyada made a covenant between himself and all the people and the king that they should be YHWH's people.

17 Then all the people went to the house of Ba'al and tore it down. His altars and his images they broke in pieces, and they killed Mattan the priest of Ba'al before the altars.

18 And Yehoyada posted watchmen for the house of YHWH under the direction of the Levitical priests and the Levites whom Dawid had organized to be in charge of the house of YHWH, to offer burnt offerings to YHWH, as it is written in the Torah of Mosheh, with rejoicing and with singing, according to the order of Dawid.

19 And he stationed gatekeepers at the gates of the house of YHWH so that no one who was in any way unclean might enter.

20 And he took the commanders of hundreds, the nobles, the governors of the people, and all the people of the land, and they brought the king down from the house of YHWH, marching through the upper gate to the king's house. And they set the king on the royal throne.

21 So all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet after Atalyahu had been put to death with the sword.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 23 is the restoration of the Davidic line after six years of Atalyah's usurpation: the priest Yehoyada coordinates the restoration, the Levites provide security, and the seven-year-old Yoash is crowned in the temple with the testimony — the Torah — placed in his hand. The community's joy at the restoration is the covenant response to the providential survival of the Davidic promise.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full. Verse 16 — Yehoyada made a covenant between himself and all the people and the king that they should be YHWH's people. The constitutional act following the restoration: the priest renews the covenant relationship between YHWH, the community, and the Davidic king. Restoration requires renewed commitment — the return to faithful leadership produces the renewal of the foundational covenant relationship.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 23 in full. Verse 18 — Yehoyada posted watchmen for the house of YHWH — to offer burnt offerings with rejoicing and with singing, according to the order of Dawid. The restoration of the temple is simultaneously the restoration of the Davidic worship order: the Davidic organizational structure for Levitical music is reinstated alongside the political restoration.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The six years of Atalyah's reign — Chapter 22:12 — during which the child king is hidden in the temple is the most concentrated narrative of covenant vulnerability in the Davidic history. The survival of the covenant promise at this moment depends on the courage of one woman — Yehosheva — and the faithfulness of one man — Yehoyada. The covenant's survival is personal before it is institutional.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 24 Yoash Did What Was Right — He Repaired the House of YHWH — The Chest Was Placed at the Gate — After Yehoyada Died Yoash Listened to the Princes — They Stoned Zekhariyahu the Son of Yehoyada — May YHWH See and Avenge

1 Yoash was seven years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Tzivyah of Be'er-Sheva.

2 And Yoash did what was right in the eyes of YHWH all the days of Yehoyada the priest.

3 Yehoyada got for him two wives, and he had sons and daughters.

4 After this Yoash decided to restore the house of YHWH.

5 And he gathered the priests and the Levites and said to them, Go out to the cities of Yehudah and gather from all Yisra'EL money to repair the house of your Creator from year to year, and see that you act quickly. But the Levites did not act quickly.

6 So the king summoned Yehoyada the chief and said to him, Why have you not required the Levites to bring in from Yehudah and Yerushalayim the contribution of Mosheh, the servant of YHWH, and of the congregation of Yisra'EL for the tent of testimony?

7 For the sons of Atalyah, that wicked woman, had broken into the house of Creator, and had also used all the dedicated things of the house of YHWH for the Ba'alim.

8 So the king commanded, and they made a chest and set it outside the gate of the house of YHWH.

9 And a proclamation was made throughout Yehudah and Yerushalayim to bring in for YHWH the tax that Mosheh the servant of Creator laid on Yisra'EL in the wilderness.

10 And all the princes and all the people rejoiced and brought their tax and dropped it into the chest until it was full.

11 And whenever the chest was brought to the king's officers by the Levites, when they saw that there was much money in it, the king's secretary and the officer of the chief priest would come and empty the chest and take it and return it to its place. Thus they did day after day, and collected money in abundance.

12 And the king and Yehoyada gave it to those who had charge of the work of the house of YHWH, and they hired masons and carpenters to restore the house of YHWH, and also workers in iron and bronze to repair the house of YHWH.

13 So those who were engaged in the work labored, and the repairing went forward in their hands, and they restored the house of Creator to its proper condition and strengthened it.

14 And when they had finished, they brought the rest of the money before the king and Yehoyada, and with it were made vessels for the house of YHWH, both for the service and for the burnt offerings, and dishes for incense and vessels of gold and silver. And they offered burnt offerings in the house of YHWH regularly all the days of Yehoyada.

15 But Yehoyada grew old and full of days, and died. He was 130 years old at his death.

16 And they buried him in the city of Dawid among the kings, because he had done good in Yisra'EL, and toward Creator and his house.

17 Now after the death of Yehoyada the princes of Yehudah came and paid homage to the king. Then the king listened to them.

18 And they abandoned the house of YHWH, the Creator of their fathers, and served the Asherim and the idols. And wrath came upon Yehudah and Yerushalayim for this guilt of theirs.

19 Yet he sent prophets among them to bring them back to YHWH. These testified against them, but they would not pay attention.

20 Then the Spirit of Creator clothed Zekhariyahu the son of Yehoyada the priest, and he stood above the people, and said to them, Thus says Creator: Why do you break the commandments of YHWH, so that you cannot prosper? Because you have forsaken YHWH, he has forsaken you.

21 But they conspired against him, and by command of the king they stoned him with stones in the court of the house of YHWH.

22 Thus Yoash the king did not remember the kindness that Yehoyada, Zekhariyahu's father, had shown him, but killed his son. And when he died, he said, May YHWH see and avenge.

23 At the end of the year the army of Syria came up against Yoash. They came to Yehudah and Yerushalayim and destroyed all the princes of the people from among the people and sent all their spoil to the king of Damascus.

24 Though the army of the Syrians had come with few men, YHWH delivered into their hand a very great army, because Yehudah had forsaken YHWH, the Creator of their fathers. Thus they executed judgment on Yoash.

25 When they had withdrawn, leaving him severely wounded, his servants conspired against him because of the blood of the son of Yehoyada the priest, and killed him on his bed. So he died, and they buried him in the city of Dawid, but they did not bury him in the tombs of the kings.

26 Those who conspired against him were Zavad the son of Shim'at the Ammonite and Yehozavad the son of Shimrit the Moabite.

27 Accounts of his sons and of the many oracles against him and of the rebuilding of the house of Creator are written in the Commentary on the Book of the Kings. And Amatziyahu his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 24 presents the pattern of conditional faithfulness: Yoash does what is right all the days of Yehoyada the priest — verse 2. The qualification reveals the dependence: the king is faithful only as long as the faithful priest lives. When Yehoyada dies at 130, Yoash becomes vulnerable to the princes' counsel and the cycle of apostasy restarts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full. Verse 22 — Yoash the king did not remember the kindness that Yehoyada his father had shown him, but killed his son. And when he died, he said, May YHWH see and avenge. The irony: the son of the man who saved Yoash's life from Atalyah is stoned by Yoash's command. Zekhariyahu's dying prayer — may YHWH see and avenge — is the prophetic curse that produces the subsequent judgment on Yoash.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 24 in full. Verse 16 — they buried Yehoyada in the city of Dawid among the kings, because he had done good in Yisra'EL, and toward Creator and his house. The highest honor given to a non-royal figure in Divrei HaYamim: burial among the kings. The priest who saved the Davidic line and guided the community for 130 years receives the honor normally reserved for royalty.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20-22 — the Spirit of Creator clothed Zekhariyahu the son of Yehoyada — they stoned him in the temple court — and when he died he said, May YHWH see and avenge. The stoning of the prophet in the temple court is the symbolic summary of the king's trajectory: from rescued in the temple to murderer in the temple. The space that preserved Yoash as a child becomes the space where Yoash commits his greatest crime.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 25 Amatziyahu Did Right But Not With a Whole Heart — He Hired 100,000 From Yisra'EL — A Man of Creator Said Do Not Let the Army of Yisra'EL Go — YHWH Is Able to Give You Much More — Why Have You Sought the Gods of Edom — Amatziyahu Would Not Listen

1 Amatziyahu was twenty-five years old when he began to reign and reigned twenty-nine years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Yehoaddan of Yerushalayim.

2 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, yet not with a whole heart.

3 And as soon as the royal power was firmly his, he killed his servants who had struck down the king his father.

4 But he did not put their children to death, according to what is written in the Torah, in the Book of Mosheh, where YHWH commanded, Fathers shall not die because of their children, nor children die because of their fathers, but each one shall die for his own sin.

5 Then Amatziyahu assembled the men of Yehudah and set them by fathers' houses under commanders of thousands and of hundreds for all Yehudah and Binyamin. He mustered those twenty years old and upward and found that they were 300,000 choice men, fit for war, able to handle spear and shield.

6 He hired also 100,000 mighty men of valor from Yisra'EL for 100 talents of silver.

7 But a man of Creator came to him and said, O king, do not let the army of Yisra'EL go with you, for YHWH is not with Yisra'EL, with all these Efrayimites.

8 But go, act, be strong for the battle. Why should you suppose that Creator will cast you down before the enemy? For Creator has power to help or to cast down.

9 And Amatziyahu said to the man of Creator, But what shall we do about the 100 talents that I have given to the army of Yisra'EL? The man of Creator answered, YHWH is able to give you much more than this.

10 Then Amatziyahu discharged the army that had come to him from Efrayim to go home again. And they became very angry with Yehudah and returned home in fierce anger.

11 But Amatziyahu took courage and led his people out and went to the Valley of Salt and struck down 10,000 men of Se'ir.

12 The men of Yehudah captured another 10,000 alive and took them to the top of a rock and threw them down from the top of the rock, and they were all dashed to pieces.

13 But the men of the army whom Amatziyahu had sent back, not letting them go with him to battle, raided the cities of Yehudah, from Samaria to Beit-Khoron, and struck down 3,000 people in them and took much spoil.

14 After Amatziyahu came from striking down the Edomites, he brought the gods of the men of Se'ir and set them up as his gods and worshiped them, making offerings to them.

15 Therefore YHWH was angry with Amatziyahu and sent to him a prophet, who said to him, Why have you sought the gods of a people who did not deliver their own people from your hand?

16 But as he was speaking, the king said to him, Have we made you a royal counselor? Stop. Why should you be struck down? So the prophet stopped, but said, I know that Creator has determined to destroy you, because you have done this and have not listened to my counsel.

17 Then Amatziyahu king of Yehudah took counsel and sent to Yoash the son of Yeho'ahaz, son of Yehu, king of Yisra'EL, saying, Come, let us look one another in the face.

18 And Yoash the king of Yisra'EL sent word to Amatziyahu king of Yehudah, The thistle on Lebanon sent to the cedar on Lebanon, saying, Give your daughter to my son for a wife, and a wild beast of Lebanon passed by and trampled down the thistle.

19 You say, See, you have struck down Edom, and your heart has lifted you up in boastfulness. But now stay at home. Why should you provoke trouble so that you fall, you and Yehudah with you?

20 But Amatziyahu would not listen, for it was of Creator, in order that he might give them into the hand of their enemies, because they had sought the gods of Edom.

21 So Yoash king of Yisra'EL went up, and he and Amatziyahu king of Yehudah faced one another in battle at Beit-Shemesh, which belongs to Yehudah.

22 And Yehudah was defeated by Yisra'EL, and every man fled to his home.

23 And Yoash king of Yisra'EL captured Amatziyahu king of Yehudah, the son of Yoash, son of Akhazyahu, at Beit-Shemesh, and brought him to Yerushalayim and broke down the wall of Yerushalayim for 400 cubits, from the Gate of Efrayim to the Corner Gate.

24 And he seized all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in the house of Creator and Oved-Edom, as well as the treasuries of the king's house, also hostages, and he returned to Samaria.

25 Amatziyahu the son of Yoash, king of Yehudah, lived fifteen years after the death of Yoash the son of Yeho'ahaz, king of Yisra'EL.

26 Now the rest of the deeds of Amatziyahu, from first to last, are they not written in the Book of the Kings of Yehudah and Yisra'EL?

27 From the time when Amatziyahu turned away from YHWH they made a conspiracy against him in Yerushalayim, and he fled to Lakhish. But they sent after him to Lakhish and put him to death there.

28 And they brought him upon horses, and he was buried with his fathers in the city of Yehudah.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 25 is the narrative of partial faithfulness — he did what was right, yet not with a whole heart — that produces incremental disaster. Amatziyahu obeys the prophet's counsel to dismiss the Israelite mercenaries but then brings the defeated Edomite gods back to Yehudah. Partial obedience cannot sustain the community.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full. Verse 15 — why have you sought the gods of a people who did not deliver their own people from your hand? The prophetic logic: Amatziyahu defeated the Edomites — the Edomite gods proved powerless to protect their own worshipers — yet Amatziyahu adopts the powerless gods as his own. The absurdity of idolatry is stated with rhetorical precision.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 25 in full. Verse 9 — YHWH is able to give you much more than this. The prophet's response to Amatziyahu's concern about the wasted 100 talents: the resource YHWH can provide exceeds any calculation of financial loss. Obedience to the prophetic word produces more than the disobedient alternative would have provided.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20 — Amatziyahu would not listen, for it was of Creator, in order that he might give them into the hand of their enemies, because they had sought the gods of Edom. The theological interpretation of stubbornness: Amatziyahu's refusal to heed the warning is attributed to divine hardening — the mechanism by which YHWH executes judgment through the community's own choices.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 26 Uziyahu Was Strong — He Sought Creator in the Days of Zekhariyahu — His Fame Spread — But When He Was Strong He Grew Proud — He Entered the Temple to Burn Incense — Leprosy Broke Out on His Forehead

1 And all the people of Yehudah took Uziyahu, who was sixteen years old, and made him king instead of his father Amatziyahu.

2 He built Elot and restored it to Yehudah, after the king slept with his fathers.

3 Uziyahu was sixteen years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-two years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Yekholyah of Yerushalayim.

4 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, according to all that his father Amatziyahu had done.

5 He set himself to seek Creator in the days of Zekhariyahu, who instructed him in the fear of Creator, and as long as he sought YHWH, Creator made him prosper.

6 He went out and made war against the Pelishtim and broke down the wall of Gat and the wall of Yavneh and the wall of Ashdod, and he built cities in the territory of Ashdod and elsewhere among the Pelishtim.

7 Creator helped him against the Pelishtim and against the Arabians who lived in Gur-Ba'al and against the Me'unites.

8 The Ammonites paid tribute to Uziyahu, and his fame spread even to the border of Egypt, for he became very strong.

9 Moreover, Uziyahu built towers in Yerushalayim at the Corner Gate and at the Valley Gate and at the Angle, and fortified them.

10 And he built towers in the wilderness and cut out many cisterns, for he had large herds, both in the Shephelah and in the plain, and he had farmers and vine dressers in the hills and in the fertile lands, for he loved the soil.

11 Moreover, Uziyahu had an army of soldiers, fit for war, in divisions according to the numbers in the muster made by Ye'iel the secretary and Ma'aseiyahu the officer, under the direction of Khananiyahu, one of the king's commanders.

12 The whole number of the heads of fathers' houses of mighty men of valor was 2,600.

13 Under their command was an army of 307,500, who could make war with mighty power, to help the king against the enemy.

14 And Uziyahu prepared for all the army shields, spears, helmets, coats of mail, bows, and stones for slinging.

15 In Yerushalayim he made engines, invented by skillful men, to be on the towers and the corners, to shoot arrows and great stones. And his fame spread far, for he was marvelously helped, till he was strong.

16 But when he was strong, he grew proud, to his destruction. For he was unfaithful to YHWH his Creator and entered the temple of YHWH to burn incense on the altar of incense.

17 But Azaryahu the priest went in after him, with eighty priests of YHWH who were men of valor,

18 And they withstood King Uziyahu and said to him, It is not for you, Uziyahu, to burn incense to YHWH, but for the priests the sons of Aharon who are consecrated to burn incense. Go out of the sanctuary, for you have done wrong, and it will bring you no honor from YHWH Creator.

19 Then Uziyahu was angry. Now he had a censer in his hand to burn incense, and when he became angry with the priests, leprosy broke out on his forehead in the presence of the priests in the house of YHWH, beside the altar of incense.

20 And Azaryahu the chief priest and all the priests looked at him, and behold, he was leprous in his forehead. And they rushed him out quickly, and he himself hurried to go out, because YHWH had struck him.

21 And King Uziyahu was a leper to the day of his death, and being a leper lived in a separate house, for he was excluded from the house of YHWH. And Yotam his son was over the king's house, governing the people of the land.

22 Now the rest of the acts of Uziyahu, from first to last, Yesha'yahu the son of Amotz the prophet wrote.

23 And Uziyahu slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers in the burial field that belonged to the kings, for they said, He is a leper. And Yotam his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 26 presents the classic trajectory of strength producing pride producing destruction. The formula of verse 5 — as long as he sought YHWH, Creator made him prosper — and verse 16 — when he was strong, he grew proud, to his destruction — is the master pattern of the Divrei HaYamim theological program.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full. Verse 18 — it is not for you, Uziyahu, to burn incense to YHWH, but for the priests the sons of Aharon who are consecrated. The priestly boundary-keeping: the priests withstand the king because the priestly function cannot be appropriated by royal authority. The separation of priestly and royal functions is a constitutional principle.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 26 in full. Verse 15 — he was marvelously helped, till he was strong. The precise sequence: divine help — growing strength — pride — destruction. The marvelous help is the foundation of the king's greatness — and the greatness is the occasion of the king's undoing. The gift becomes the trap.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22 — the rest of the acts of Uziyahu, Yesha'yahu the son of Amotz the prophet wrote. Uziyahu's reign is the historical context of Yesha'yahu's call — Yesha'yahu 6:1. The year that King Uziyahu died is the year Yesha'yahu saw YHWH in high and exalted vision. The king's death is the prophet's commissioning moment.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 27 Yotam Did What Was Right — He Did Not Enter the Temple — He Built the Upper Gate — The People Still Followed Corrupt Practices — He Became Mighty — Yotam Slept With His Fathers

1 Yotam was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Yerushah the daughter of Tzadok.

2 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH according to all that his father Uziyahu had done — except he did not enter the temple of YHWH. But the people still followed corrupt practices.

3 He built the upper gate of the house of YHWH and did much building on the wall of Ofel.

4 Moreover, he built cities in the hill country of Yehudah, and in the forests he built fortresses and towers.

5 He fought with the king of the Ammonites and prevailed against them. And the Ammonites paid him that year 100 talents of silver, and 10,000 cors of wheat and 10,000 of barley. The Ammonites paid him the same amount in the second and the third years.

6 So Yotam became mighty, because he ordered his ways before YHWH his Creator.

7 Now the rest of the acts of Yotam, and all his wars and his ways, behold, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Yisra'EL and Yehudah.

8 He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim.

9 And Yotam slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the city of Dawid, and Akhaz his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 27 is the briefest king narrative in Divrei HaYamim — nine verses — for the king who did right without the single qualification of entering the temple. Faithful unremarkable reign receives brief documentation. The silence is itself a theological statement.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full. Verse 6 — Yotam became mighty, because he ordered his ways before YHWH his Creator. The formula for the faithful king: ordering of ways before YHWH — putting the community's relationship with YHWH in the correct order — produces might. Covenant-keeping produces covenant blessing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 27 in full. Verse 2 — he did not enter the temple of YHWH. But the people still followed corrupt practices. The king can order his own ways before YHWH without succeeding in reforming the people. Individual faithfulness does not automatically produce communal faithfulness.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The brevity of Chapter 27 is itself a theological statement: unremarkable faithfulness requires less explanation than spectacular failure. The compiler who spends twenty-three verses on Uziyahu's dramatic collapse gives nine verses to Yotam's quieter, steadier, less eventful faithfulness.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 28 Akhaz Did Not Do Right — He Made Offerings in the Valley of Ben-Hinnom — He Closed the Doors of the House of YHWH — Oded Said You Have Killed Them in a Rage — They Returned the Captives — YHWH Was Humbling Yehudah

1 Akhaz was twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Yerushalayim. And he did not do what was right in the eyes of YHWH, as his father Dawid had done,

2 But he walked in the ways of the kings of Yisra'EL. He even made metal images for the Ba'alim,

3 And he made offerings in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom and burned his sons as an offering, according to the abominations of the nations whom YHWH drove out before the people of Yisra'EL.

4 And he sacrificed and made offerings on the high places and on the hills and under every green tree.

5 Therefore YHWH his Creator gave him into the hand of the king of Syria, who defeated him and took captive a great number of his people and brought them to Damascus. He was also given into the hand of the king of Yisra'EL, who struck him with great force.

6 For Pekakh the son of Remal'yahu killed 120,000 from Yehudah in one day, all of them men of valor, because they had forsaken YHWH, the Creator of their fathers.

7 And Zikhri, a mighty man of Efrayim, killed Ma'aseiyahu the king's son and Azrikam the commander of the palace and Elkana the next in authority to the king.

8 The men of Yisra'EL took captive 200,000 of their relatives, women, sons, and daughters. They also took much spoil from them and brought the spoil to Samaria.

9 But a prophet of YHWH was there, whose name was Oded, and he went out to meet the army that came to Samaria and said to them, Behold, because YHWH, the Creator of your fathers, was angry with Yehudah, he gave them into your hand, but you have killed them in a rage that has reached up to heaven.

10 And now you intend to subjugate the people of Yehudah and Yerushalayim, male and female, as your slaves. Have you not sins of your own against YHWH your Creator?

11 Now hear me, and send back the captives from your relatives whom you have taken, for the fierce wrath of YHWH is upon you.

12 Certain chiefs also of the men of Efrayim, Azaryahu the son of Yehohanan, Berekhiyahu the son of Meshillemot, Yekhizkiyahu the son of Shallum, and Amasa the son of Hadlai, stood up against those who were coming from the war

13 And said to them, You shall not bring the captives in here, for you propose to bring upon us guilt against YHWH in addition to our sins and our guilt, for our guilt is already great, and there is fierce wrath against Yisra'EL.

14 So the armed men left the captives and the spoil before the princes and all the assembly.

15 And the men who have been mentioned by name rose and took the captives, and with the spoil they clothed all who were naked among them. They clothed them, gave them sandals, provided them with food and drink, and anointed them, and carrying all the feeble among them on donkeys, they brought them to their kinsfolk at Yericho, the city of palms. Then they returned to Samaria.

16 At that time King Akhaz sent to the king of Assyria for help.

17 For the Edomites had again invaded and defeated Yehudah and carried away captives.

18 And the Pelishtim had made raids on the cities in the Shephelah and the Negev of Yehudah, and had taken Beit-Shemesh, Ayalon, Gederot, Sokho with its villages, Timnah with its villages, and Gimzo with its villages. And they settled there.

19 For YHWH humbled Yehudah because of Akhaz king of Yisra'EL, for he had made Yehudah act sinfully and had been very unfaithful to YHWH.

20 So Tilgat-Pilneser king of Assyria came against him and afflicted him instead of strengthening him.

21 For Akhaz took a portion from the house of YHWH and the house of the king and of the princes, and gave tribute to the king of Assyria, but it did not help him.

22 In the time of his distress he became yet more faithless to YHWH — this same King Akhaz.

23 For he sacrificed to the gods of Damascus that had defeated him and said, Because the gods of the kings of Syria helped them, I will sacrifice to them that they may help me. But they were the ruin of him and of all Yisra'EL.

24 And Akhaz gathered together the vessels of the house of Creator and cut in pieces the vessels of the house of Creator, and he shut the doors of the house of YHWH, and he made himself altars at every corner in Yerushalayim.

25 And in every city of Yehudah he made high places to make offerings to other gods, provoking to anger YHWH, the Creator of his fathers.

26 Now the rest of his acts and all his ways, from first to last, behold, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Yehudah and Yisra'EL.

27 And Akhaz slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the city, in Yerushalayim, for they did not bring him into the tombs of the kings of Yisra'EL. And Khizkiyahu his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 28 is the nadir of the Yehudah monarchy before the exile — Akhaz burned his sons in the Valley of Hinnom, shut the temple doors, and in distress became yet more faithless. The remarkable story of the northern prophet Oded and the compassionate chiefs — verses 9-15 — is the bright light within the chapter's darkness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 28 in full. Verse 15 — they clothed the captives, gave them sandals, provided food and drink, and anointed them, and carrying all the feeble among them on donkeys, they brought them to their kinsfolk at Yericho. The northern Israelites' compassionate treatment of Yehudah's captives is the most striking act of covenant brotherhood in the entire divided-kingdom narrative.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 28 in full. Verse 22-23 — in the time of his distress he became yet more faithless to YHWH. For he sacrificed to the gods of Damascus that had defeated him — saying, I will sacrifice to them that they may help me. But they were the ruin of him. The theological perversity: Akhaz interprets Syrian victory as evidence of Syrian gods' power and switches allegiance to the gods that defeated him.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19 — YHWH humbled Yehudah because of Akhaz king of Yisra'EL. The designation of Akhaz as king of Yisra'EL — not king of Yehudah — is the most severe condemnation a king of Yehudah can receive: he is identified with the apostate north rather than the faithful Davidic south. Akhaz has forfeited the Davidic covenant identity through complete abandonment of YHWH.

CHAPTERS 29 THROUGH 36 — KHIZKIYAHU YOSHIYAHU AND THE EXILE

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 29 Khizkiyahu Opened the Doors of the House of YHWH — He Said Our Fathers Were Unfaithful — The Levites Consecrated Themselves — They Removed the Uncleanness — Khizkiyahu Rejoiced — The Thing Came About Suddenly

1 Khizkiyahu began to reign when he was twenty-five years old, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Aviyah the daughter of Zekhariyahu.

2 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, according to all that Dawid his father had done.

3 In the first year of his reign, in the first month, he opened the doors of the house of YHWH and repaired them.

4 He brought in the priests and the Levites and assembled them in the square on the east

5 And said to them, Hear me, Levites. Now consecrate yourselves, and consecrate the house of YHWH, the Creator of your fathers, and carry out the filth from the Holy Place.

6 For our fathers have been unfaithful and have done what was evil in the sight of YHWH our Creator. They have forsaken him and have turned away their faces from the habitation of YHWH and turned their backs.

7 They also shut the doors of the vestibule and put out the lamps and have not burned incense or offered burnt offerings in the Holy Place to the Creator of Yisra'EL.

8 Therefore the wrath of YHWH came on Yehudah and Yerushalayim, and he has made them an object of dread, of astonishment, and of hissing, as you see with your own eyes.

9 For behold, our fathers have fallen by the sword, and our sons and our daughters and our wives are in captivity for this.

10 Now it is in my heart to make a covenant with YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, in order that his fierce anger may turn away from us.

11 My sons, do not now be negligent, for YHWH has chosen you to stand in his presence, to minister to him and to be his ministers and make offerings to him.

12 Then the Levites arose, Makhat the son of Amasai and Yo'el the son of Azaryahu, of the sons of the Kohathites. And of the sons of Merari, Kish the son of Avdi and Azaryahu the son of Yehallelel. And of the Gershonites, Yo'akh the son of Zimmah and Eden the son of Yo'akh.

13 And of the sons of Elizafan, Shimri and Ye'iel. And of the sons of Asaf, Zekhariyahu and Mattanyahu.

14 And of the sons of Heman, Yekhiel and Shim'i. And of the sons of Yedutun, Shemayahu and Uzzi'el.

15 They gathered their brothers and consecrated themselves and went in as the king had commanded, by the words of YHWH, to cleanse the house of YHWH.

16 The priests went into the inner part of the house of YHWH to cleanse it, and they brought out all the uncleanness that they found in the temple of YHWH into the court of the house of YHWH. And the Levites took it and carried it out to the brook Kidron.

17 They began to consecrate on the first day of the first month, and on the eighth day of the month they came to the vestibule of YHWH. Then for eight days they consecrated the house of YHWH, and on the sixteenth day of the first month they finished.

18 Then they went in to Khizkiyahu the king and said, We have cleansed all the house of YHWH, the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, and the table for the showbread and all its utensils.

19 All the utensils that King Akhaz discarded in his reign when he was unfaithful, we have made ready and consecrated, and behold, they are before the altar of YHWH.

20 Then Khizkiyahu the king rose early and gathered the officials of the city and went up to the house of YHWH.

21 And they brought seven bulls, seven rams, seven lambs, and seven male goats for a sin offering for the kingdom and for the sanctuary and for Yehudah. And he commanded the priests the sons of Aharon to offer them on the altar of YHWH.

22 So they slaughtered the bulls, and the priests received the blood and threw it against the altar. And they slaughtered the rams, and their blood was thrown against the altar. And they slaughtered the lambs, and their blood was thrown against the altar.

23 Then the male goats for the sin offering were brought before the king and the assembly, and they laid their hands on them,

24 And the priests slaughtered them and made a sin offering with their blood on the altar, to make atonement for all Yisra'EL. For the king commanded that the burnt offering and the sin offering should be made for all Yisra'EL.

25 And he stationed the Levites in the house of YHWH with cymbals, harps, and lyres, according to the commandment of Dawid and of Gad the king's seer and of Natan the prophet, for the commandment was from YHWH through his prophets.

26 The Levites stood with the instruments of Dawid, and the priests with the trumpets.

27 Then Khizkiyahu commanded that the burnt offering be offered on the altar. And when the burnt offering began, the song to YHWH began also, and the trumpets, accompanied by the instruments of Dawid king of Yisra'EL.

28 And all the assembly worshiped, and the singers sang, and the trumpeters sounded. All this continued until the burnt offering was finished.

29 When the offering was finished, the king and all who were present with him bowed themselves and worshiped.

30 And Khizkiyahu the king and the officials commanded the Levites to sing praises to YHWH with the words of Dawid and of Asaf the seer. And they sang praises with gladness, and they bowed down and worshiped.

31 Then Khizkiyahu said, You have now consecrated yourselves to YHWH. Come near and bring sacrifices and thank offerings to the house of YHWH. And the assembly brought sacrifices and thank offerings, and all who were of a willing heart brought burnt offerings.

32 The number of the burnt offerings that the assembly brought was 70 bulls, 100 rams, and 200 lambs. All these were for a burnt offering to YHWH.

33 And the consecrated offerings were 600 bulls and 3,000 sheep.

34 But the priests were too few and could not flay all the burnt offerings, so until other priests had consecrated themselves, their brothers the Levites helped them, until the work was finished — for the Levites were more upright in heart than the priests in consecrating themselves.

35 Besides the great number of burnt offerings, there were the fat of the peace offerings, and there were the drink offerings for the burnt offerings. Thus the service of the house of YHWH was restored.

36 And Khizkiyahu and all the people rejoiced because Creator had provided for the people, for the thing came about suddenly.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 29 opens the Khizkiyahu narrative — the most extensively documented king in Divrei HaYamim after Dawid and Shelomoh. The first act of his reign — opening the temple doors in the first month of his first year — is the reversal of Akhaz's last act. The reformation begins with verse 10: it is in my heart to make a covenant with YHWH.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 29 in full. Verse 36 — Khizkiyahu and all the people rejoiced because Creator had provided for the people, for the thing came about suddenly. The joy of the temple restoration: the speed of the reformation — accomplished in sixteen days — is evidence of divine enablement. The community had been without proper worship for the entire reign of Akhaz; the restoration comes suddenly.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 29 in full. Verse 34 — the Levites were more upright in heart than the priests in consecrating themselves. The frank assessment of the clergy's relative readiness: the Levites outpaced the priests in eagerness for the restoration. The compiler does not protect the priestly reputation at the expense of accuracy.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25 — he stationed the Levites according to the commandment of Dawid and of Gad the king's seer and of Natan the prophet, for the commandment was from YHWH through his prophets. The restoration reinstates not only the physical worship structures but the Davidic musical organization — grounded in the three-fold authorization of Dawid, Gad, and Natan. The musical tradition is rooted in prophetic authority.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 30 Khizkiyahu Sent Letters to All Yisra'EL — Come to the House of YHWH — Do Not Be Like Your Fathers — Some Men Laughed — A Very Great Assembly Gathered — Creator Heard and Healed the People — There Was Great Joy in Yerushalayim

1 Khizkiyahu sent to all Yisra'EL and Yehudah, and wrote letters also to Efrayim and Menasheh, that they should come to the house of YHWH at Yerushalayim to keep the Passover to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

2 For the king and his princes and all the assembly in Yerushalayim had taken counsel to keep the Passover in the second month —

3 For they could not keep it at that time because the priests had not consecrated themselves in sufficient number, nor had the people assembled in Yerushalayim —

4 The plan seemed right to the king and all the assembly.

5 So they decreed to make a proclamation throughout all Yisra'EL, from Be'er-Sheva to Dan, that the people should come and keep the Passover to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, at Yerushalayim. For they had not kept it as often as prescribed.

6 So couriers went throughout all Yisra'EL and Yehudah with letters from the king and his princes, as the king had commanded, saying, O people of Yisra'EL, return to YHWH, the Creator of Avraham, Yitzkhak, and Yisra'EL, that he may turn again to the remnant of you who have escaped from the hand of the kings of Assyria.

7 Do not be like your fathers and your brothers, who were faithless to YHWH Creator of their fathers, so that he made them a desolation, as you see.

8 Do not now be stiff-necked as your fathers were, but yield yourselves to YHWH and come to his sanctuary, which he has consecrated forever, and serve YHWH your Creator, that his fierce anger may turn away from you.

9 For if you return to YHWH, your brothers and your children will find compassion with their captors and return to this land. For YHWH your Creator is gracious and merciful and will not turn away his face from you, if you return to him.

10 So the couriers went from city to city through the country of Efrayim and Menasheh, and as far as Zevulun, but they laughed at them and mocked them.

11 However, some men of Asher, of Menasheh, and of Zevulun humbled themselves and came to Yerushalayim.

12 The hand of Creator was also on Yehudah to give them one heart to do what the king and the princes commanded by the word of YHWH.

13 And many people came together in Yerushalayim to keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread in the second month, a very great assembly.

14 They set to work and removed the altars that were in Yerushalayim, and all the altars for burning incense they took away and threw into the brook Kidron.

15 And they slaughtered the Passover lamb on the fourteenth day of the second month. And the priests and the Levites were ashamed, so that they consecrated themselves and brought burnt offerings into the house of YHWH.

16 They took their accustomed posts according to the Torah of Mosheh the man of Creator. The priests threw the blood that they received from the hand of the Levites.

17 For there were many in the assembly who had not consecrated themselves. Therefore the Levites had to slaughter the Passover lamb for everyone who was not clean, to consecrate it to YHWH.

18 For a majority of the people, many of them from Efrayim, Menasheh, Yissachar, and Zevulun, had not cleansed themselves, yet they ate the Passover otherwise than as prescribed. For Khizkiyahu had prayed for them, saying, May the good YHWH pardon everyone

19 Who sets his heart to seek Creator, YHWH the Creator of his fathers, even though not according to the sanctuary's rules of cleanness.

20 And YHWH heard Khizkiyahu and healed the people.

21 And the people of Yisra'EL who were present at Yerushalayim kept the Feast of Unleavened Bread seven days with great gladness, and the Levites and the priests praised YHWH day by day, singing with all their might to YHWH.

22 And Khizkiyahu spoke encouragingly to all the Levites who showed good skill in the service of YHWH. So they ate the food of the feast for seven days, sacrificing peace offerings and giving thanks to YHWH, the Creator of their fathers.

23 Then the whole assembly agreed together to keep the feast for another seven days. So they kept it for another seven days with gladness.

24 For Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah gave the assembly 1,000 bulls and 7,000 sheep for offerings, and the princes gave the assembly 1,000 bulls and 10,000 sheep. And the priests consecrated themselves in great numbers.

25 The whole assembly of Yehudah, and the priests and the Levites, and the whole assembly that came out of Yisra'EL, and the sojourners who came out of the land of Yisra'EL, and the sojourners who lived in Yehudah, rejoiced.

26 So there was great joy in Yerushalayim, for since the time of Shelomoh the son of Dawid king of Yisra'EL there had been nothing like this in Yerushalayim.

27 Then the priests and the Levites arose and blessed the people, and their voice was heard, and their prayer came to his holy habitation in heaven.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 30 is the Passover of Khizkiyahu — the re-gathering of northern and southern communities for the foundational festival of the Exodus. The response is mixed: most in Efrayim and Menasheh laugh — some humble themselves and come. The prayer of verse 18-20 — healed the people — demonstrates that YHWH responds to heart orientation more than ritual precision.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 30 in full. Verse 18-20 — may the good YHWH pardon everyone who sets his heart to seek Creator, even though not according to the sanctuary's rules of cleanness. And YHWH heard Khizkiyahu and healed the people. YHWH's response to the king's generous intercession: the heart set toward YHWH is honored even when ritual precision is lacking.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 30 in full. Verse 26 — there was great joy in Yerushalayim, for since the time of Shelomoh there had been nothing like this. The Passover of Khizkiyahu is the greatest communal celebration since the Solomonic golden age: the compiler explicitly connects the revival of Khizkiyahu to the covenant standard of that era.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — YHWH your Creator is gracious and merciful and will not turn away his face from you, if you return to him. The covenant invitation letter's theological core: the Shemot 34 character formula applied to the national call to return. The invitation is grounded in YHWH's established character — gracious and merciful — as the assurance that the return will be met with welcome.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 31 All Yisra'EL Broke the Pillars and Cut Down the Asherim — Khizkiyahu Appointed the Divisions of the Priests — He Commanded the People to Give the Portion — The Contributions Piled Up in Heaps — You Have Done Right Before YHWH

1 Now when all this was finished, all Yisra'EL who were present went out to the cities of Yehudah and broke in pieces the pillars and cut down the Asherim and broke down the high places and the altars throughout all Yehudah and Binyamin, and in Efrayim and Menasheh, until they had destroyed them all. Then all the people of Yisra'EL returned to their cities, every man to his possession.

2 And Khizkiyahu appointed the divisions of the priests and of the Levites, division by division, each according to his service, the priests and the Levites, for burnt offerings and peace offerings, to minister in the gates of the camp of YHWH and to give thanks and praise.

3 The contribution of the king from his own possessions was for the burnt offerings: the burnt offerings of morning and evening, and the burnt offerings for the Shabbatot, the new moons, and the appointed feasts, as it is written in the Torah of YHWH.

4 And he commanded the people who lived in Yerushalayim to give the portion due to the priests and the Levites, that they might give themselves to the Torah of YHWH.

5 As soon as the command was spread abroad, the people of Yisra'EL gave in abundance the firstfruits of grain, wine, oil, honey, and of all the produce of the field. And they brought in abundantly the tithe of everything.

6 And the people of Yisra'EL and Yehudah who lived in the cities of Yehudah also brought in the tithe of cattle and sheep, and the tithe of the dedicated things that had been dedicated to YHWH their Creator, and laid them in heaps.

7 In the third month they began to pile up the heaps, and finished them in the seventh month.

8 When Khizkiyahu and the princes came and saw the heaps, they blessed YHWH and his people Yisra'EL.

9 And Khizkiyahu questioned the priests and the Levites about the heaps.

10 Azaryahu the chief priest, who was of the house of Tzadok, answered him, Since they began to bring the contributions into the house of YHWH, we have eaten and had enough and have plenty left, for YHWH has blessed his people, so that we have this large amount left.

11 Then Khizkiyahu commanded them to prepare chambers in the house of YHWH, and they prepared them.

12 And they faithfully brought in the contributions, the tithes, and the dedicated things. The chief officer in charge of them was Konaniyahu the Levite, with Shim'i his brother as second.

13 And Yekhiel, Azazyahu, Nakhat, Asahel, Yerimot, Yozavad, Eli'el, Yissachar, Makhbanai, and Benayahu were overseers assisting Konaniyahu and Shim'i his brother, by appointment of Khizkiyahu the king and Azaryahu the chief officer of the house of Creator.

14 And Kore the son of Yimna the Levite, keeper of the east gate, was over the freewill offerings to Creator, to apportion the contribution reserved for YHWH and the most holy offerings.

15 Eden, Minyamin, Yeshua, Shemayahu, Amaryahu, and Shekhanyahu were faithfully assisting him in the cities of the priests, to distribute to their brothers, old and young alike, by divisions,

16 Except those enrolled by genealogy, males from three years old and upward — all who entered the house of YHWH as the duty of each day required — for their service according to their offices, by their divisions.

17 The enrollment of the priests was according to their fathers' houses. The Levites from twenty years old and upward were enrolled according to their offices, by their divisions.

18 They were enrolled with all their little children, their wives, their sons, and their daughters, the whole assembly, for they were faithful in keeping themselves holy.

19 And for the sons of Aharon, the priests, who were in the fields of the pasturelands of their cities, there were men in the several cities who were designated by name to distribute portions to every male among the priests and to everyone among the Levites who was enrolled.

20 Thus Khizkiyahu did throughout all Yehudah, and he did what was good and right and faithful before YHWH his Creator.

21 And every work that he undertook in the service of the house of Creator and in accordance with the Torah and the commandments, seeking his Creator, he did with all his heart, and prospered.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 31 documents the institutional restoration of the tithe and offering system — the economic foundation of Levitical and priestly service. The heaps of contributions are the visible evidence of the community's response to the revival: the reformation produces generous giving.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 31 in full. Verse 21 — every work that he undertook in the service of the house of Creator and in accordance with the Torah and the commandments, seeking his Creator, he did with all his heart, and prospered. The triple characteristic of the faithful king: service in the house of Creator — obedience to the Torah and commandments — seeking of Creator. These elements together with whole-heartedness produce prosperity.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 31 in full. Verse 10 — since they began to bring the contributions, we have eaten and had enough and have plenty left, for YHWH has blessed his people, so that we have this large amount left. The overflow of the tithe: generous giving to the Levites produces surplus. The blessing of YHWH on covenant faithfulness produces more than the need.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — all Yisra'EL who were present went out throughout Yehudah and Binyamin, and in Efrayim and Menasheh. The geographical scope of the reformation extends beyond the boundaries of Yehudah into the northern territories. The Passover of Chapter 30 produced a reunion that makes possible a comprehensive reformation across both kingdoms.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 32 Sennakherib King of Assyria Came — Khizkiyahu Stopped the Water — He Built Up the Wall — Be Strong and Courageous — With Him Is an Arm of Flesh But With Us Is YHWH — The Angel of YHWH Cut Down the Mighty Warriors — Khizkiyahu's Heart Was Lifted Up

1 After these things and these acts of faithfulness, Sennakherib king of Assyria came and invaded Yehudah and encamped against the fortified cities, thinking to win them for himself.

2 And when Khizkiyahu saw that Sennakherib had come and intended to fight against Yerushalayim,

3 He planned with his officers and his mighty men to stop the water of the springs that were outside the city, and they helped him.

4 A great many people were gathered, and they stopped all the springs and the brook that flowed through the land, saying, Why should the kings of Assyria come and find much water?

5 He set to work resolutely and built up all the wall that was broken down and raised towers upon it, and outside it he built another wall, and he strengthened the Millo in the city of Dawid. He also made weapons and shields in abundance.

6 And he set combat commanders over the people and gathered them together to him in the square at the gate of the city and spoke encouragingly to them, saying,

7 Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or dismayed before the king of Assyria and all the horde that is with him, for there is one greater with us than with him.

8 With him is an arm of flesh, but with us is YHWH our Creator, to help us and to fight our battles. And the people took confidence from the words of Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah.

9 After this, Sennakherib king of Assyria, who was besieging Lakhish with all his forces, sent his servants to Yerushalayim to Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah and to all the people of Yehudah who were in Yerushalayim, saying,

10 Thus says Sennakherib king of Assyria, On what are you trusting, that you endure the siege in Yerushalayim?

11 Is not Khizkiyahu misleading you, that he may give you over to die by famine and by thirst, when he tells you, YHWH our Creator will deliver us from the hand of the king of Assyria?

12 Has not this same Khizkiyahu taken away his high places and his altars and commanded Yehudah and Yerushalayim, You shall worship before one altar and on it you shall make your offerings?

13 Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of other lands? Were the gods of the nations of those lands at all able to deliver their lands out of my hand?

14 Who among all the gods of those nations that my fathers devoted to destruction was able to deliver his people from my hand, that your Creator should be able to deliver you from my hand?

15 Now, therefore, do not let Khizkiyahu deceive you or mislead you in this fashion, and do not believe him, for no god of any nation or kingdom has been able to deliver his people from my hand or from the hand of my fathers. How much less will your Creator deliver you out of my hand.
