16 His servants said still more against YHWH Creator and against his servant Khizkiyahu.

17 And he wrote letters to cast contempt on YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, and to speak against him, saying, Like the gods of the nations of the lands who have not delivered their people from my hands, so the Creator of Khizkiyahu will not deliver his people from my hand.

18 And they shouted it with a loud voice in the language of Yehudah to the people of Yerushalayim who were on the wall, to frighten and terrify them, in order that they might take the city.

19 And they spoke of the Creator of Yerushalayim as they spoke of the gods of the peoples of the earth, which are the work of men's hands.

20 Then Khizkiyahu the king and Yesha'yahu the prophet, the son of Amotz, prayed because of this and cried to heaven.

21 And YHWH sent an angel, who cut off all the mighty warriors and commanders and officers in the camp of the king of Assyria. So he returned with shame of face to his own land. And when he came into the house of his god, some of his own sons struck him down there with the sword.

22 So YHWH saved Khizkiyahu and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim from the hand of Sennakherib king of Assyria and from the hand of all his enemies, and he provided for them on every side.

23 And many brought gifts to YHWH to Yerushalayim and precious things to Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah, so that he was exalted in the sight of all nations from that time onward.

24 In those days Khizkiyahu became sick and was at the point of death, and he prayed to YHWH, and he answered him and gave him a sign.

25 But Khizkiyahu did not make return according to the benefit done to him, for his heart was proud. Therefore wrath came upon him and Yehudah and Yerushalayim.

26 But Khizkiyahu humbled himself for the pride of his heart, both he and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, so that the wrath of YHWH did not come upon them in the days of Khizkiyahu.

27 And Khizkiyahu had very great riches and honor, and he made himself treasuries for silver, for gold, for precious stones, for spices, for shields, and for all kinds of costly vessels.

28 Storehouses also for the yield of grain, wine, and oil, and stalls for all kinds of cattle, and sheepfolds.

29 He likewise provided cities for himself, and flocks and herds in abundance, for Creator had given him very great possessions.

30 This same Khizkiyahu closed the upper outlet of the waters of Gikkon and directed them down to the west side of the city of Dawid. And Khizkiyahu prospered in all his works.

31 And so in the matter of the envoys of the princes of Babylon, who had been sent to him to inquire about the sign that had been done in the land, Creator left him to himself, in order to test him and to know all that was in his heart.

32 Now the rest of the acts of Khizkiyahu and his good deeds, behold, they are written in the vision of Yesha'yahu the prophet the son of Amotz, in the Book of the Kings of Yehudah and Yisra'EL.

33 And Khizkiyahu slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the upper part of the tombs of the sons of Dawid, and all Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim did him honor at his death. And Menasheh his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 32 compresses the Sennakherib crisis into its theological essence: Khizkiyahu and Yesha'yahu prayed and cried to heaven — and YHWH sent an angel who cut off all the mighty warriors. Verse 7-8 — with him is an arm of flesh, but with us is YHWH our Creator — is the theological summary of every battle in Divrei HaYamim.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 32 in full. Verse 25-26 — Khizkiyahu's heart was proud. Therefore wrath came upon him. But Khizkiyahu humbled himself, so that the wrath of YHWH did not come upon them. The final test of Khizkiyahu: even the best king succumbs to pride — but recovers through humility. The pattern of Uziyahu is averted by timely covenant humility.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 32 in full. Verse 31 — Creator left him to himself, in order to test him and to know all that was in his heart. The Babylonian envoy episode is given one verse with the theological interpretation: YHWH withdrew to test Khizkiyahu's heart. The test of prosperity complements the test of adversity: the king's character is revealed both when the enemy threatens and when envoys flatter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — with him is an arm of flesh, but with us is YHWH our Creator, to help us and to fight our battles. The theological contrasts: flesh versus Creator, human military versus divine military. The contrast is not between large armies and small armies but between dependent creaturely strength and sovereign divine power. The whole of the Divrei HaYamim theology of battle is compressed into this single verse.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 33 Menasheh Did Much Evil — He Rebuilt the High Places — YHWH Spoke But He Would Not Listen — YHWH Brought the Commanders of Assyria — Menasheh Humbled Himself — He Knew That YHWH Is Creator — Amon Did Evil

1 Menasheh was twelve years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-five years in Yerushalayim.

2 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, according to the abominations of the nations whom YHWH drove out before the people of Yisra'EL.

3 For he rebuilt the high places that his father Khizkiyahu had broken down, and he erected altars to the Ba'alim, and made Asherim, and worshiped all the host of heaven and served them.

4 And he built altars in the house of YHWH, of which YHWH had said, In Yerushalayim shall my name be forever.

5 And he built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of YHWH.

6 And he burned his sons as an offering in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom, and used fortune-telling and omens and sorcery, and dealt with mediums and with necromancers. He did much evil in the sight of YHWH, provoking him to anger.

7 And the carved image of the idol that he had made he set in the house of Creator, of which Creator said to Dawid and to Shelomoh his son, In this house and in Yerushalayim, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Yisra'EL, I will put my name forever.

8 And I will not again remove the foot of Yisra'EL from the land that I appointed for your fathers, if only they will be careful to do all that I have commanded them, all the Torah, the statutes, and the rules given through Mosheh.

9 Menasheh led Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim astray, to do more evil than the nations whom YHWH had destroyed before the people of Yisra'EL.

10 YHWH spoke to Menasheh and to his people, but they paid no attention.

11 Therefore YHWH brought upon them the commanders of the army of the king of Assyria, who captured Menasheh with hooks and bound him with chains of bronze and brought him to Babylon.

12 And when he was in distress, he entreated the favor of YHWH his Creator and humbled himself greatly before the Creator of his fathers.

13 He prayed to him, and Creator was moved by his entreaty and heard his plea and brought him again to Yerushalayim into his kingdom. Then Menasheh knew that YHWH is Creator.

14 Afterward he built an outer wall for the city of Dawid west of Gikkon, in the valley, and for the entrance into the Fish Gate, and carried it around Ofel, and raised it to a very great height. He also put commanders of the army in all the fortified cities in Yehudah.

15 And he took away the foreign gods and the idol from the house of YHWH, and all the altars that he had built on the mountain of the house of YHWH and in Yerushalayim, and he threw them outside the city.

16 He also restored the altar of YHWH and offered on it sacrifices of peace offerings and of thanksgiving, and he commanded Yehudah to serve YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

17 Nevertheless, the people still sacrificed at the high places, but only to YHWH their Creator.

18 Now the rest of the acts of Menasheh, and his prayer to his Creator, and the words of the seers who spoke to him in the name of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, behold, they are in the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL.

19 And his prayer, and how Creator was moved by his entreaty, and all his sin and his faithlessness, and the sites on which he built high places and set up the Asherim and the images, before he humbled himself, behold, they are written in the Chronicles of Hozai.

20 So Menasheh slept with his fathers, and they buried him in his house. And Amon his son reigned in his place.

21 Amon was twenty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned two years in Yerushalayim.

22 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, as Menasheh his father had done. Amon sacrificed to all the images that Menasheh his father had made, and served them.

23 And he did not humble himself before YHWH, as Menasheh his father had humbled himself, but this same Amon incurred guilt more and more.

24 And his servants conspired against him and put him to death in his house.

25 But the people of the land struck down all those who had conspired against King Amon. And the people of the land made Yoshiyahu his son king in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 33 contains the most theologically surprising event in Divrei HaYamim: the repentance and restoration of Menasheh — the worst king in the history of Yehudah. The compiler includes material about Menasheh's repentance absent from Melakhim Bet. The possibility of repentance for even the most egregious covenant-breaker is the theological statement of Menasheh's restoration.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 33 in full. Verse 12-13 — when he was in distress, he entreated the favor of YHWH his Creator and humbled himself greatly. He prayed to him, and Creator was moved by his entreaty and heard his plea and brought him again to Yerushalayim. Then Menasheh knew that YHWH is Creator. The knowledge that comes through distress: Menasheh's acknowledgment of YHWH is the product of the extremity of Babylonian captivity.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 33 in full. Verse 23 — Amon did not humble himself before YHWH, as Menasheh his father had humbled himself, but Amon incurred guilt more and more. The contrast between father and son: Menasheh who sinned greatly but humbled himself deeply — and Amon who sinned and did not humble himself. The trajectory is determined not by initial character but by response to covenant discipline.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Menasheh repentance narrative — absent from Melakhim Bet — is the compiler's most distinctive theological contribution in Divrei HaYamim Bet. The parallel account in Melakhim Bet presents Menasheh as irredeemably wicked. The compiler's inclusion of the prayer and restoration is the statement that no king — and by extension no person — is beyond the reach of repentance.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 34 Yoshiyahu Did What Was Right — He Began to Seek the Creator — He Purged Yehudah — They Found the Book of the Torah — The King Tore His Clothes — Khuldah the Prophetess Said — Your Heart Was Tender

1 Yoshiyahu was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned thirty-one years in Yerushalayim.

2 And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, and walked in the ways of Dawid his father, and did not turn aside to the right hand or to the left.

3 For in the eighth year of his reign, while he was yet a boy, he began to seek the Creator of Dawid his father, and in the twelfth year he began to purge Yehudah and Yerushalayim of the high places, the Asherim, and the carved and the metal images.

4 And they chopped down the altars of the Ba'alim in his presence, and he cut down the incense altars that stood above them. And he broke in pieces the Asherim and the carved and the metal images, and he made dust of them and scattered it over the graves of those who had sacrificed to them.

5 He also burned the bones of the priests on their altars and cleansed Yehudah and Yerushalayim.

6 And in the cities of Menasheh, Efrayim, and Shim'on, and as far as Naftali, in their ruins all around,

7 He broke down the altars and beat the Asherim and the images into powder and cut down all the incense altars throughout all the land of Yisra'EL. Then he returned to Yerushalayim.

8 Now in the eighteenth year of his reign, when he had purged the land and the house, he sent Shafan the son of Atzalyahu, and Ma'aseiyahu the governor of the city, and Yo'akh the son of Yokha'haz, the recorder, to repair the house of YHWH his Creator.

9 They came to Khilkiyahu the high priest and gave him the money that had been brought into the house of Creator, which the Levites, the keepers of the threshold, had collected from Menasheh and Efrayim and from all the remnant of Yisra'EL and from all Yehudah and Binyamin and from the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.

10 And they gave it to the workmen who were working in the house of YHWH. And the workmen who were working in the house of YHWH gave it for repairing and restoring the house.

11 They gave it to the carpenters and the builders to buy quarried stone, and timber for binders and beams for the buildings that the kings of Yehudah had let go to ruin.

12 And the men did the work faithfully. Over them were set Yakhat and Ovadyahu, Levites of the sons of Merari, and Zekhariyahu and Meshullam, of the sons of the Kohathites, to have oversight. The Levites, all who were skillful with instruments of music,

13 Were over the burden-bearers and directed all who did work in every kind of service. And some of the Levites were scribes and officials and gatekeepers.

14 While they were bringing out the money that had been brought into the house of YHWH, Khilkiyahu the priest found the Book of the Torah of YHWH given through Mosheh.

15 Then Khilkiyahu answered and said to Shafan the secretary, I have found the Book of the Torah in the house of YHWH. And Khilkiyahu gave the book to Shafan.

16 Shafan brought the book to the king, and further reported to the king, All that was committed to your servants they are doing.

17 They have emptied out the money that was found in the house of YHWH and have given it into the hand of the overseers and the workmen.

18 Then Shafan the secretary told the king, Khilkiyahu the priest has given me a book. And Shafan read from it before the king.

19 And when the king heard the words of the Torah, he tore his clothes.

20 And the king commanded Khilkiyahu, Akhi'kam the son of Shafan, Avdon the son of Mikhah, Shafan the secretary, and Asayah the king's servant, saying,

21 Go, inquire of YHWH for me and for those who are left in Yisra'EL and in Yehudah, concerning the words of the book that has been found. For great is the wrath of YHWH that is poured out on us, because our fathers have not kept the word of YHWH, to do according to all that is written in this book.

22 So Khilkiyahu and those whom the king had sent went to Khuldah the prophetess, the wife of Shallum the son of Tokhath, son of Khasra, keeper of the wardrobe — now she lived in Yerushalayim in the second quarter — and they spoke to her to that effect.

23 And she said to them, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Tell the man who sent you to me,

24 Thus says YHWH, Behold, I will bring disaster upon this place and upon its inhabitants, all the curses that are written in the book that was read before the king of Yehudah.

25 Because they have forsaken me and have made offerings to other gods, that they might provoke me to anger with all the works of their hands, therefore my wrath will be poured out on this place and will not be quenched.

26 But to the king of Yehudah, who sent you to inquire of YHWH, thus shall you say to him, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Regarding the words that you have heard,

27 Because your heart was tender and you humbled yourself before Creator when you heard his words against this place and its inhabitants, and you have humbled yourself before me and have torn your clothes and wept before me, I also have heard you, declares YHWH.

28 Behold, I will gather you to your fathers, and you shall be gathered to your grave in peace, and your eyes shall not see all the disaster that I will bring upon this place and its inhabitants. And they brought back word to the king.

29 Then the king sent and gathered together all the elders of Yehudah and Yerushalayim.

30 And the king went up to the house of YHWH, with all the men of Yehudah and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim and the priests and the Levites, all the people both great and small. And he read in their hearing all the words of the Book of the Covenant that had been found in the house of YHWH.

31 And the king stood in his place and made a covenant before YHWH, to walk after YHWH and to keep his commandments and his testimonies and his statutes, with all his heart and all his soul, to perform the words of the covenant that were written in this book.

32 Then he made all who were present in Yerushalayim and in Binyamin stand to it. And the inhabitants of Yerushalayim did according to the covenant of Creator, the Creator of their fathers.

33 And Yoshiyahu took away all the abominations from all the territory that belonged to the people of Yisra'EL and made all who were present in Yisra'EL serve YHWH their Creator. All his days they did not turn away from following YHWH, the Creator of their fathers.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 34 is the Yoshiyahu narrative — the last great reforming king before the exile. The finding of the Book of the Torah in the temple is the pivotal discovery. The king's immediate response — tearing his clothes — is the sign of the tender heart that the prophetess Khuldah identifies as the reason for his personal protection from coming judgment.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 34 in full. Verse 27 — because your heart was tender and you humbled yourself before Creator when you heard his words — and you have humbled yourself before me and have torn your clothes and wept before me, I also have heard you. The tender heart is the most important characteristic in the compiler's evaluation of kings. Khuldah's word to Yoshiyahu is the most personal prophetic word given to any king in Divrei HaYamim.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 34 in full. Verse 22 — they went to Khuldah the prophetess. The delegation sent to inquire of YHWH goes to a woman — Khuldah — rather than to Yesha'yahu or Yirmeyahu, both of whom were active during Yoshiyahu's reign. The authorization of the Book of the Torah comes through a woman's prophetic voice. The institutional covenant reform of the greatest reforming king is grounded in a woman's testimony.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — in the eighth year of his reign, while he was yet a boy, he began to seek the Creator of Dawid his father. Yoshiyahu's seeking of YHWH begins at age sixteen — without any recorded prophetic prompt, without any recorded crisis, without any external pressure. The covenant seeking of Yoshiyahu is the most purely initiated in the post-Shelomoh monarchy: a young king who seeks YHWH simply because that is what the king of Yisra'EL should do.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 35 Yoshiyahu Kept a Passover — He Set the Priests in Their Duties — The Levites Taught All Yisra'EL — No Passover Like It Had Been Kept Since Shemu'el — Yoshiyahu Went Out to Fight — He Did Not Listen to the Words of Nekho From the Mouth of Creator — Yoshiyahu Died

1 Yoshiyahu kept a Passover to YHWH in Yerushalayim. And they slaughtered the Passover lamb on the fourteenth day of the first month.

2 He appointed the priests to their offices and encouraged them in the service of the house of YHWH.

3 And he said to the Levites who taught all Yisra'EL and who were holy to YHWH, Put the holy ark in the house that Shelomoh the son of Dawid, king of Yisra'EL, built. You need not carry it on your shoulders. Now serve YHWH your Creator and his people Yisra'EL.

4 Prepare yourselves according to your fathers' houses by your divisions, following the written directions of Dawid king of Yisra'EL and the document of his son Shelomoh.

5 And stand in the Holy Place according to the groupings of the fathers' houses of your brothers the lay people, and according to the division of the Levites by fathers' house.

6 And slaughter the Passover lamb, and consecrate yourselves, and prepare for your brothers, to do according to the word of YHWH by Mosheh.

7 Then Yoshiyahu contributed to the lay people, as Passover offerings for all who were present, lambs and young goats from the flock to the number of 30,000, and 3,000 bulls. These were from the king's possessions.

8 And his officials contributed willingly to the people, to the priests, and to the Levites. Khilkiyahu, Zekhariah, and Yekhiel, the chief officers of the house of Creator, gave to the priests for the Passover offerings 2,600 Passover lambs and 300 bulls.

9 Konaniyahu also, and Shemayahu and Netanel his brothers, and Khashabyahu and Ye'iel and Yozavad, the chiefs of the Levites, gave to the Levites for the Passover offerings 5,000 lambs and young goats and 500 bulls.

10 When the service had been prepared for, the priests stood in their place, and the Levites in their divisions according to the king's command.

11 And they slaughtered the Passover lamb, and the priests threw the blood that they received from them while the Levites flayed the animals.

12 And they set aside the burnt offerings that they might distribute them according to the groupings of the fathers' houses of the lay people, to offer to YHWH, as it is written in the Book of Mosheh. And so they did with the bulls.

13 And they roasted the Passover lamb with fire according to the rule, and they boiled the holy offerings in pots, in cauldrons, and in pans, and carried them quickly to all the lay people.

14 And afterward they prepared for themselves and for the priests, because the priests, the sons of Aharon, were busy offering the burnt offerings and the fat parts until night. So the Levites prepared for themselves and for the priests, the sons of Aharon.

15 The singers, the sons of Asaf, were in their place according to the command of Dawid, and Asaf, and Heman, and Yedutun the king's seer. And the gatekeepers were at each gate. They did not need to depart from their service, for their brothers the Levites prepared for them.

16 So all the service of YHWH was prepared that day, to keep the Passover and to offer burnt offerings on the altar of YHWH, according to the command of King Yoshiyahu.

17 And the people of Yisra'EL who were present kept the Passover at that time, and the Feast of Unleavened Bread seven days.

18 No Passover like it had been kept in Yisra'EL since the days of Shemu'el the prophet. None of the kings of Yisra'EL had kept such a Passover as was kept by Yoshiyahu, and the priests and the Levites, and all Yehudah and Yisra'EL who were present, and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim.

19 In the eighteenth year of the reign of Yoshiyahu this Passover was kept.

20 After all this, when Yoshiyahu had prepared the temple, Nekho king of Egypt went up to fight at Karkhemish on the Euphrates, and Yoshiyahu went out to meet him.

21 But he sent envoys to him, saying, What have we to do with each other, king of Yehudah? I am not coming against you this day, but against the house with which I am at war. And Creator has told me to hurry. Stop opposing Creator who is with me, lest he destroy you.

22 Nevertheless, Yoshiyahu did not turn away from him, but disguised himself in order to fight with him. He did not listen to the words of Nekho from the mouth of Creator, but came to fight in the Valley of Megiddo.

23 And the archers shot King Yoshiyahu. And the king said to his servants, Take me away, for I am badly wounded.

24 So his servants took him out of the chariot and carried him in his second chariot and brought him to Yerushalayim. And he died and was buried in the tombs of his fathers. All Yehudah and Yerushalayim mourned for Yoshiyahu.

25 Yirmeyahu also uttered a lament for Yoshiyahu, and all the singing men and singing women have spoken of Yoshiyahu in their laments to this day. They made these a rule in Yisra'EL. Behold, they are written in the Laments.

26 Now the rest of the acts of Yoshiyahu, and his good deeds according to what is written in the Torah of YHWH,

27 And his acts, first and last, behold, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Yisra'EL and Yehudah.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 35 is the Passover of Yoshiyahu — greater than any Passover since the days of Shemu'el — verse 18. The death of Yoshiyahu at Megiddo — from not listening to the words of Nekho that came from the mouth of Creator — is the tragic irony of the book: the greatest reforming king dies because he failed to discern the divine word when it came through a pagan king.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 35 in full. Verse 18 — no Passover like it had been kept in Yisra'EL since the days of Shemu'el the prophet. The Passover standard rooted in Shemu'el rather than Mosheh suggests a covenant restoration to the pre-monarchic era of the judges — the earliest period of communal covenant life in the land.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 35 in full. Verse 24-25 — all Yehudah and Yerushalayim mourned for Yoshiyahu. Yirmeyahu also uttered a lament for Yoshiyahu. The communal mourning is the measure of the king's significance: the community mourns in proportion to the loss. Yoshiyahu's death is the most deeply mourned royal death in Divrei HaYamim.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22 — Yoshiyahu did not listen to the words of Nekho from the mouth of Creator. The tragic theological statement: the king whose heart was tender enough to weep over the Book of the Torah fails to recognize the divine word when it comes through an enemy king. The ability to discern YHWH's voice in unexpected sources is the test Yoshiyahu fails — the only significant failure of the book's most faithful king.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 36 Yeo'akhaz — Yehoiakim — Yehoiachin — Tzidkiyahu — He Did Evil and Stiffened His Neck — The Wrath of YHWH Rose Against His People — They Burned the House of Creator — The Land Kept Its Shabbatot — Until the Kingdom of Persia — That the Word of YHWH by the Mouth of Yirmeyahu Might Be Fulfilled

1 The people of the land took Yeo'akhaz the son of Yoshiyahu and made him king in his father's place in Yerushalayim.

2 Yeo'akhaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months in Yerushalayim.

3 Then the king of Egypt deposed him at Yerushalayim and laid on the land a tribute of a hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.

4 And the king of Egypt made Elyakim his brother king over Yehudah and Yerushalayim, and changed his name to Yehoiakim. But Nekho took Yeo'akhaz his brother and carried him to Egypt.

5 Yehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Yerushalayim. He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH his Creator.

6 Against him came up Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon and bound him in chains to take him to Babylon.

7 Nevukhadnetzar also carried part of the vessels of the house of YHWH to Babylon and put them in his palace in Babylon.

8 Now the rest of the acts of Yehoiakim, and the abominations that he did, and what was found against him, behold, they are written in the Book of the Kings of Yisra'EL and Yehudah. And Yehoiachin his son reigned in his place.

9 Yehoiachin was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months and ten days in Yerushalayim. He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH.

10 In the spring of the year King Nevukhadnetzar sent and brought him to Babylon, with the precious vessels of the house of YHWH, and made his brother Tzidkiyahu king over Yehudah and Yerushalayim.

11 Tzidkiyahu was twenty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Yerushalayim.

12 He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH his Creator. He did not humble himself before Yirmeyahu the prophet, who spoke from the mouth of YHWH.

13 He also rebelled against King Nevukhadnetzar, who had made him swear by Creator. He stiffened his neck and hardened his heart against turning to YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

14 All the officers of the priests and the people likewise were exceedingly unfaithful, following all the abominations of the nations. And they polluted the house of YHWH that he had consecrated in Yerushalayim.

15 YHWH, the Creator of their fathers, sent persistently to them by his messengers, because he had compassion on his people and on his dwelling place.

16 But they kept mocking the messengers of Creator, despising his words and scoffing at his prophets, until the wrath of YHWH rose against his people, until there was no remedy.

17 Therefore he brought up against them the king of the Chaldeans, who killed their young men with the sword in the house of their sanctuary and had no compassion on young man or virgin, old man or aged. He gave them all into his hand.

18 And all the vessels of the house of Creator, great and small, and the treasures of the house of YHWH, and the treasures of the king and of his princes, all these he brought to Babylon.

19 And they burned the house of Creator and broke down the wall of Yerushalayim and burned all its palaces with fire and destroyed all its precious vessels.

20 He took into exile in Babylon those who had escaped from the sword, and they became servants to him and to his sons until the establishment of the kingdom of Persia,

21 To fulfill the word of YHWH by the mouth of Yirmeyahu, until the land had enjoyed its Shabbatot. All the days that it lay desolate it kept Shabbat, to fulfill seventy years.

22 Now in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of YHWH by the mouth of Yirmeyahu might be fulfilled, YHWH stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, so that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom and also put it in writing:

23 Thus says Cyrus king of Persia, YHWH, the Creator of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth, and he has charged me to build him a house at Yerushalayim, which is in Yehudah. Whoever is among you of all his people, may YHWH his Creator be with him. Let him go up.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 36 is the final chapter of the covenant historical narrative — the accelerating collapse of the final four kings and the exile to Babylon. The theological heart of the chapter is verse 15-16: YHWH sent persistently by his messengers because he had compassion on his people and on his dwelling place. But they kept mocking the messengers of Creator, despising his words and scoffing at his prophets, until the wrath of YHWH rose against his people, until there was no remedy.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 36 in full. Verse 21 — to fulfill the word of YHWH by the mouth of Yirmeyahu, until the land had enjoyed its Shabbatot. All the days that it lay desolate it kept Shabbat, to fulfill seventy years. The theological interpretation of the exile's duration: the land itself observes the Shabbat rests that the community neglected. The desolation of the exile is the land's Shabbat sabbatical — Vayikra 26:34-35 fulfilled.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 36 in full. Verse 22-23 — in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of YHWH by the mouth of Yirmeyahu might be fulfilled, YHWH stirred up the spirit of Cyrus. Let him go up. The entire historical narrative of Divrei HaYamim — from Adam in Chapter 1 of Aleph to Cyrus in Chapter 36 of Bet — ends with an open invitation: let him go up. The covenant history ends not with closure but with the beginning of the return.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23 — let him go up. The final words of Divrei HaYamim are not a conclusion but an invitation. The entire covenant historical record — from the creation genealogies through the monarchy, the temple, the reformations, the exiles — ends at the point of departure, not arrival. The covenant community that reads these words is invited to go up. The covenant compiler's final statement is not about the past but about the covenant future: whoever is among you of all his people, may YHWH his Creator be with him. Let him go up.
